text.title: security-sast Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for security-sast. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Block PR merge on CRITICAL and HIGH SAST findings only; expose MEDIUM and LOW as informational annotations. Tighten thresholds over time as baseline noise decreases.
rule.rationale: Blocking on every finding causes alert fatigue and trains developers to ignore or bypass the gate. Start narrow and expand coverage as the team becomes comfortable with the tool's signal-to-noise ratio.

rule.statement: Pin scanner image digests and rule-pack versions in CI; bump them on a defined schedule (e.g., monthly), never silently via floating tags.
rule.rationale: Silent rule-pack updates change what is flagged, causing previously-passing PRs to fail without any code change — making CI untrustworthy.

rule.statement: Set a baseline on first integration to ignore pre-existing issues; only gate on new issues introduced in the PR diff.
rule.rationale: Retrofitting SAST into a legacy codebase without a baseline produces thousands of findings in the first run, blocking all new work. Address technical security debt separately with a dedicated sprint.
